title: Dell's $51B AI Backlog — Thermal & Connector Pick-and-Shovel Play
summary: Dell's blowout Q1 FY27 print (May 28) lifted its FY27 AI server guide to $60B and pushed AI backlog to a record $51.3B; the rack-scale thermal and high-speed interconnect suppliers underneath that build (Vertiv, Amphenol) are already running 1.2x+ book-to-bill and have not yet repriced for the new ramp.
catalyst: On May 28, 2026, Dell reported $16.1B of Q1 FY27 AI server revenue (+757% YoY), $24.4B in new AI orders, and ended the quarter with a record $51.3B AI backlog — and raised FY27 AI server revenue guidance from $50B to $60B [1][2]. The print sent DELL +32% on May 29 (its best day ever) and confirms hyperscaler/sovereign-AI demand is materially larger than analyst models. Every NVIDIA Blackwell rack Dell ships at 132 kW density requires liquid cooling and copious high-speed interconnects.
thesis: The market has repriced Dell, but the second-derivative beneficiaries — the rack-scale liquid cooling and the 800G/1.6T copper-and-fiber backbone — have not fully re-rated. Vertiv co-developed the reference cooling architecture for NVIDIA's GB200 NVL72 platform [5] and is one of two dominant players sized to deliver rack-scale CDUs at hyperscaler volumes. Amphenol holds ~33% share of AI data-center interconnects and printed 81% organic IT-datacom growth in Q1 with a 1.24 book-to-bill [3]. Dell's 757% AI server growth is the upstream signal; the downstream thermal/connector content per rack moves with it.
body_md:
## Setup

Dell's Q1 FY2027 earnings (May 28, 2026) reset the AI infrastructure conversation. The company reported $43.8B in total revenue (+88% YoY) and $16.1B of AI server revenue, up 757% from the year-ago quarter [1]. It booked $24.4B of new AI orders, finished the quarter with a record **$51.3B AI backlog**, and raised its FY27 AI server revenue guide to roughly **$60B** (from a prior $50B) and total revenue to $165–169B [1][2]. CFO commentary and CEO Michael Dell's deck both emphasized that demand continues to exceed supply, with memory and standard components — not customer appetite — as the binding constraint [2]. Dell's AI customer count crossed 5,000 and the pipeline remains a multiple of backlog after the order conversion.

This is not just a Dell story. Dell's print is the cleanest single data point of the quarter that the hyperscaler + sovereign + enterprise AI capex curve is steeper than consensus. Independent trackers now peg the top five US hyperscalers' 2026 capex at **$660–690B**, up roughly 36% YoY, with ~75% directly tied to AI infrastructure [6]. That spend has to land on physical rack-scale hardware, and the bill of materials for a single NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack (132 kW, 72 Blackwell GPUs, 1.8 TB/s per-GPU NVLink) is dominated by two non-silicon line items: thermal management and high-speed interconnects [5].

## Why Now

Three things make this actionable today rather than a generic "AI tailwind" call:

1. **The demand signal is now confirmed at the OEM layer.** Until Dell's print, the AI ramp was visible primarily in NVIDIA's results, which sit one layer up the stack. Dell — alongside Supermicro's $12.7B Q2 revenue — confirms that orders are flowing through to the system integrators and out to delivered racks. That's the trigger event for the consumables: cooling distribution units (CDUs), rear-door heat exchangers, high-density busways, copper twinax cables, fiber connectors, and DC power shelves.
2. **The picks-and-shovels have already pre-printed the demand.** Vertiv's Q1 2026 (reported April 22, 2026) delivered $2.65B revenue (+30% organic), 20.8% adjusted operating margin (+430 bps), and a backlog of **$12.45B**, up ~81% YoY [4]. Management raised full-year 2026 revenue guidance to **$13.5–14.0B** with adjusted operating margin guided to 22–23%. Amphenol's Q1 2026 (April 29, 2026) printed record sales of **$7.6B** (+58% reported, +33% organic), record orders of **$9.4B** (+78% YoY), a **1.24 book-to-bill**, and adjusted diluted EPS of **$1.06** (+68%) [3]. These prints predate Dell's $60B revision — so consensus FY27 numbers for both names do not yet incorporate the implied lift.
3. **The screener has both names as BUYs already.** The IOWN screen rates Vertiv at 82/100 (Growth sleeve) and Amphenol at 81/100 (Growth sleeve) with AI Resilience scores of 10 and 9 respectively — both flagged as direct beneficiaries of the AI buildout rather than at-risk businesses [4][3].

## Company-Specific Edge

### Vertiv (VRT) — the rack-scale thermal franchise

Vertiv co-developed with NVIDIA the complete power-and-cooling reference architecture for the GB200 NVL72 platform, including a 7 MW reference design that reduces implementation time by up to 50% [5]. The platform supports up to 132 kW per rack — well beyond what air cooling can handle — making liquid cooling effectively mandatory for Blackwell-class systems. Vertiv's CoolPhase CDU line, rear-door heat exchangers, and high-density busways are now embedded in the NVIDIA reference design that Dell, Supermicro, HPE, and the ODMs deploy.

The screener flags 41.8% ROE, 22.7% YoY revenue growth, and a 252% organic-order surge in Q4 2025 [4]. After the Q1 2026 print, the backlog stands at $12.45B against trailing-twelve-month revenue of ~$11B — roughly 12–15 months of forward revenue visibility before any incremental Dell-driven orders. The CommScope of cooling moats: every rack the customer adds increases switching cost on the next rack, because the CDU and busway topology has already been engineered to Vertiv's spec.

### Amphenol (APH) — the silent backbone

Amphenol commands roughly **33% market share in AI data-center interconnects** [3]. The bill of materials per Blackwell rack includes high-speed copper twinax (NVLink/NVSwitch), 800G/1.6T optical pluggables, power connectors, and increasingly Linear Pluggable Optics where Amphenol is a category leader. The January 2026 **$10.5B CommScope CCS acquisition** adds an estimated $4.1B of 2026 fiber-optic revenue and deepens the in-rack fiber moat at a moment when NVL72 → NVL576 transitions push optical content per rack materially higher [3].

Amphenol's structural edge is operational: 130+ decentralized business units with full P&L authority, run by CEO Adam Norwitt (30-year veteran, recently elevated to Chairman). The model has compounded EPS at ~21% annually for two decades. Q1 2026 adjusted operating margin was 27.5% — exceptional for a hardware franchise — and Q4 2025 EPS grew 77% YoY [3].

## Numbers That Matter

| Metric | VRT | APH |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 2026 revenue | $2.65B (+30% organic) | $7.62B (+33% organic) |
| Q1 2026 backlog / orders | $12.45B backlog (+81% YoY) | $9.4B orders, 1.24 b-to-b |
| Adj. operating margin | 20.8% (+430 bps) | 27.5% |
| FY2026 revenue guide | $13.5–14.0B | Implied $30B+ |
| ROE (screener) | 41.8% | 36.9% |
| Forward P/E (screener) | 31.5x | 23.9x |
| Profit margin (screener) | 13.0% | 18.5% |
| AI Resilience (screener) | 10/10 | 9/10 |
| Inspire Impact (faith) | +74 (ALIGNED) | +58 (ALIGNED) |

The key cross-check: Dell's $51.3B AI backlog at typical infrastructure-content ratios implies roughly $3–5B of incremental rack-scale thermal demand and $4–6B of incremental high-speed interconnect demand over the conversion window — a meaningful tailwind on top of the $13.75B and $30B+ revenue bases above, and one that consensus models for FY27 do not yet capture.

## Bear Case

Three honest risks. First, **valuation is rich**. VRT's 31.5x forward P/E and APH's 23.9x forward P/E both demand sustained 25%+ growth; any quarter where book-to-bill slips below 1.0 will trigger a multi-multiple compression. Second, **hyperscaler concentration**. The same Microsoft/Amazon/Google/Meta block that makes the thesis also makes both names vulnerable to a single capex-pause headline. Meta's 9% drop in April when it raised 2026 capex guidance to $125–145B is a reminder that the market now grades AI spend on returns, not commitments. Third, **supply chain ricochets**. Both companies depend on transformers, copper, fiber, and specialty connectors whose lead times have stretched. A bottleneck (e.g., the recent Hormuz-related metals shock that has bid up copper) could constrain conversion of backlog into revenue, capping the upside even as orders flow in.

The China exposure deserves a specific call-out for Amphenol — material production presence in China makes the franchise exposed to a 5–10% cost increase under any tariff escalation. The CommScope integration also adds execution risk over the next 4–6 quarters.

## Conclusion

Dell's May 28 print is the cleanest confirmation yet that AI server volumes are pulling forward, not slowing. The two highest-content non-silicon line items per rack — thermal management and high-speed interconnects — flow to a small number of credible suppliers. Vertiv and Amphenol both reported pre-Dell Q1 results that already validate the demand; neither set of consensus estimates incorporates the $51.3B backlog signal yet. With both names on the BUY list and AI Resilience scores at the top of the screener, this is the asymmetric setup that the macro day produced: the OEM has rerated, the suppliers have not.

ticker_rationale.VRT: Co-developer with NVIDIA on the GB200 NVL72 reference cooling architecture and a $12.45B Q1 2026 backlog (+81% YoY) provide 12+ months of forward revenue visibility before any Dell-driven incremental orders [4][5]. Screener flags 41.8% ROE, 30% organic Q1 growth, and a 10/10 AI Resilience score.
ticker_rationale.APH: 33% share of AI data center interconnects and Q1 2026 orders of $9.4B (1.24 book-to-bill, +78% YoY) confirm the connector bill-of-materials per rack is scaling ahead of the AI build [3]. Adjusted operating margin of 27.5% and a 30-year-veteran CEO running a decentralized model are best-in-class operationally.

risks: Hyperscaler capex pause: a single Microsoft/Amazon/Google/Meta guidance cut on 2026 AI spend would compress book-to-bill at both names within one quarter and trigger 20-30% multiple compression — the Meta -9% reaction on its $125-145B 2026 capex raise shows the market is grading returns, not commitments.

Supply chain bottleneck: transformer, copper, and specialty-component lead times remain stretched (linked to the unresolved Hormuz metals shock); either name could miss revenue conversion targets even with orders growing, capping near-term upside.
Valuation reset risk: VRT at 31.5x forward and APH at 23.9x forward leave no margin for a guide-down — a single missed quarter on margin or order growth could re-rate both names sharply.

Amphenol-specific: CommScope CCS integration is APH's largest-ever deal ($10.5B); cultural or margin friction over the next 4-6 quarters could weigh on the consolidated P&L, and material China manufacturing presence is exposed to any tariff escalation.
Vertiv-specific: 81.9% D/E ratio and limited public-company history through a downturn — the cyclicality test has not yet been administered to current management.
invalidation: Dell reports a Q2 FY27 AI backlog below $45B (vs $51.3B Q1 exit) without an offsetting revenue conversion, signaling demand peak rather than backlog working down — close the trade.
VRT Q2 2026 organic orders growth comes in below 0% YoY or book-to-bill drops below 1.0 — invalidates the rack-scale thermal acceleration thesis.
APH IT datacom segment organic growth falls below 25% YoY in any quarter through Q4 2026 (vs the 81% Q1 print) — would mark the end of the AI interconnect content-per-rack ramp.
Either name's forward P/E re-rates above 40x without a corresponding earnings revision higher — the asymmetric setup converts to a momentum trade and the entry edge is gone.
Two consecutive hyperscalers cut their 2026 capex guidance by >10% in their next reporting cycle — close both positions on the second cut.

sources.title: Dell Technologies Delivers First Quarter Fiscal 2027 Financial Results
Amphenol Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Results
Vertiv Holdings Co Form 8-K Exhibit 99.1 — Q1 2026 Results
Vertiv codevelops with NVIDIA complete power and cooling blueprint for NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 platform

metrics_note: 2026-06-22: APH screener recommendation downgraded BUY → HOLD on metric refresh; thesis intact pending Q3 print.
